title: iOS: merge Activity into the unified Feed (project rows, attention-first)
description:
Product decision (2026-07-20): collapse the separate Activity tab into the multi-pairing Feed so there is ONE surface. User chose 'project rows, attention-first': keep one row per project per machine (today's Feed), fold in Activity's value (unread tracking + attention highlighting + error surfacing + event-level deep-link), and REMOVE the Activity tab.

WHY: post multi-pairing, Feed is multi-machine but Activity was still single-machine (primary store only) and the two reverse-chronological lists overlapped. Feed already sorts by newest AgentEvent and aggregates across machines; it just lacks unread + error surfacing + per-event deep-link.

DESIGN:
- FeedItem carries the project's latest AgentEvent (kind: needsInput/finished/error + deepLink.sessionId) in addition to the RollupDot. RollupDot has no finished/error; those come from EventKind.
- Unread: a persisted per-item (pairingId+projectId) last-seen watermark, fed by ALL machines' agentEvents (aggregate, not just primary). A row is unread when its latest event occurredAtMs > its watermark. Feed tab shows the unread badge (tab-feed-unread-badge); opening a row marks it read. Reuse ActivityStore's durable/dedup/persist machinery, repurposed to multi-machine + per-item watermarks.
- Sort: online-before-offline, then attention (live needs-input OR latest event needsInput/error) before non-attention, then activityMs desc. => needs-input, error, finished/idle, offline.
- Row: unread dot + machine chip + rollup dot + event-derived summary (needsInput preview / finished summary / error message); error styled red; needs-input accent (existing); offline dimmed+badge+retry (existing). Tap a needs-input/error row -> deep-link to that session (b8d.12 route pattern, carry pairingId+sessionId); else open the project's sessions.
- Remove the Activity tab (.activity from AppTab/CustomTabBar/leadingTabs), delete ActivityFeedView/ActivityFeedModel, drop MainTabView's .activity case + markViewed-on-select; move unread badge to Feed. Keep -uitest-fixture-activity working (now seeds the feed's unread).
- Migrate UI tests: ActivityUITests -> feed unread tests; NavigationUITests/DocScreenshotUITests tab-activity refs; verify ChatTranscript 'activity' refs are transcript items (unrelated).
- PITFALLS learned this session: do NOT synchronously mutate @Observable stores in View init/body (defer with Task{@MainActor} - caused a launch hang); ensure new tappable controls sit above the tab bar (env-propagated tabBarHeight); -uitest-reset must clear new persisted state.

ACCEPTANCE: one Feed tab (no Activity); feed rows show unread dots + attention highlight + error + per-machine chip across all Macs; unread badge on Feed tab; tap needs-input->that session; unread persists across launches and clears on open; ship gate green (527+ unit tests, full UI suite green with retry, Rust untouched); CHANGELOG updated.
close_reason: Activity merged into Feed: multi-machine unread+attention+error+deep-link on project rows; Activity tab removed; 535 unit + full UI suite green (retry)

title: iOS: aggregated feed store (interleave by recency, per-instance tag, offline cache)
description:
Build the aggregation layer for the unified feed: merge projects/sessions from all paired machines into one flat list, interleaved by recency, each item tagged with its instance; offline machines still appear with last-known items, dimmed.

FILES / ANCHORS:
- ios/FlightDeckRemote/Transport/TransportStore.swift - today a single @Observable global store (snapshot/transcripts/git/shell/command handles), flat and not namespaced by machine. Keep a per-instance TransportStore per client (IOS-3).
- New FeedStore that: collects each instance's snapshot; caches last-known snapshot per pairingId (so offline machines still render); produces one interleaved-by-recency list of feed items, each carrying pairingId + resolved display name (from PairingsStore) + online/offline flag.

WHAT TO BUILD:
- Reactive aggregation over the per-instance stores. Persist last-known snapshot per pairingId so a cold start with an offline machine still shows something. Offline = that instance's client is not connected/authed.
design: Interleave feed items by most-recent activity across machines. Each item resolves its machine chip from PairingsStore (override > desktop name). Offline items render dimmed with an "offline" badge and a retry affordance. The feed is the aggregated INDEX; per-session/chat/monitor detail state stays per-instance (IOS-10). Keep aggregation reactive to per-instance store updates so the feed live-updates as instances sync.
acceptance_criteria: Feed shows interleaved items from 2+ machines by recency with correct machine chips; offline machine's items appear dimmed from cache; feed updates live as instances sync; last-known snapshots survive cold start; CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: FeedStore: recency-interleaved aggregation, per-pairing offline cache, reactive; 458 unit tests green

title: iOS: transport coordinator (N clients, foreground connect-all / background teardown)
description:
Today one TransportClient (an actor) holds a single record/channel/e2e/supervisor, and one TransportStore is built by TransportStoreFactory.makeDefault. Introduce a coordinator that owns up to N (<= cap) TransportClients, one per pairing, each connecting to its own relayURL. Connect all while the app is foreground; tear all down on background (APNs push takes over).

FILES / ANCHORS:
- ios/FlightDeckRemote/Transport/TransportClient.swift - actor fields at ~80-89 (single record/channel/e2e/supervisor); runSupervisor ~195 loads one record; connects record.relayURL ~219; sends auth_response with pairingIds: [record.pairingId] ~312; drops any envelope whose pairingId != record.pairingId ~409-412. Keep the client single-pairing; just make it instantiable many times.
- ios/FlightDeckRemote/Features/Monitor/TransportStoreFactory.swift (makeDefault ~46) - currently builds ONE TransportStore + TransportClient + PairingRecordStore.

WHAT TO BUILD:
- A TransportCoordinator that instantiates one TransportClient (+ its per-instance TransportStore, see IOS-4/IOS-10) per PairedInstance from PairingsStore, each using its own PairingRecord (IOS-1) and relayURL.
- Lifecycle: react to PairingsStore add/remove (spin a client up / stop+dispose it); on scenePhase foreground -> start all supervisors, background -> stop all and close sockets.
design: Bounded fan-out (<= cap ~4) keeps N sockets acceptable. All clients REUSE the phone's shared DeviceIdentity (Secure Enclave) + KeyAgreement keys - do NOT mint per-pairing device keys. Ensure clean teardown (cancel supervisors, close URLSessionWebSocketTask) to avoid leaks, and guarantee no lingering sockets when backgrounded. Handle runtime add (pair new machine) and remove (unpair) by starting/stopping the corresponding client only.
acceptance_criteria: With 2+ pairings, foregrounding starts one live client per machine (own relay); backgrounding tears them down with no lingering sockets; runtime add/remove spins clients up/down; shared device keys reused across clients; CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: TransportCoordinator owning N per-pairing clients; scenePhase connect-all/teardown; runtime add/remove; 448 unit tests green

title: iOS: PairedInstance model + persisted PairingsStore
description:
Replace the boolean/single-device pairing state with a persisted list of paired instances plus per-instance metadata.

FILES / ANCHORS:
- ios/FlightDeckRemote/Features/Pairing/PairingStore.swift (~line 48). Today: isPaired: Bool persisted in UserDefaults key "agency.neworange.flightdeck.remote.isPaired", plus an in-memory pairedDevice: PairedDevice?.
- PairedDevice (ios/FlightDeckRemote/Features/Pairing/PairingService.swift:24): { pairingId, peerName, pairedAt }.
- RealPairingService.pair (ios/FlightDeckRemote/Transport/RealPairingService.swift:63, saves record at ~148).

WHAT TO BUILD:
- Define PairedInstance: { pairingId, machineNameFromDesktop: String?, userOverrideName: String?, relayURL: URL, mutePush: Bool, pairedAt: Date, lastKnownOnline: Bool }. Display name = userOverrideName ?? machineNameFromDesktop ?? fallback.
- Turn PairingStore into an @Observable store over [PairedInstance] with: add(instance), remove(pairingId), setOverrideName(pairingId,_), setMutePush(pairingId,_), list. Persist the metadata list (UserDefaults/JSON).
- Update RealPairingService.pair to APPEND a new PairedInstance rather than replace (cap enforced in IOS-5).
design: Secrets and cursors stay in the Keychain store (IOS-1); PairedInstance holds only non-secret display/prefs metadata, keyed by the same pairingId (join on pairingId). PairingsStore is the single source of truth for "which machines am I paired with", consumed by the router (IOS-5), feed (IOS-4/6), transport coordinator (IOS-3), push (IOS-8), and settings/unpair (IOS-9). Keep it reactive (@Observable) so those layers update on add/remove.
acceptance_criteria: PairingsStore persists [PairedInstance] across launches; pairing appends an instance; display-name precedence (override>desktop>fallback) correct; mute/override setters persist; unit tests; CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: PairedInstance + @Observable PairingsStore persisted; append-on-pair; 441 unit tests green

title: Multi-pairing: phone-initiated unpair/revoke (protocol+relay+desktop)
description:
Unpairing one machine from the phone must delete the local pairing record AND revoke the pairing on the relay so the desktop learns it is unpaired (product decision), without affecting the phone's OTHER pairings. First verify whether a phone->relay unpair/revoke frame already exists; if not, add one that drops PairingMembers for a given PairingId and notifies the desktop peer.

FILES / ANCHORS:
- remote/protocol/src/relay.rs - add/confirm an unpair/revoke frame (phone -> relay) carrying the PairingId, plus a notification frame (relay -> desktop).
- remote/relay/src/session.rs - handle the revoke: verify the authenticated requester (DeviceId) is a member of the pairing, remove the pairing from the store, drop routing-registry entries (remote/relay/src/router.rs), notify the desktop peer.
- remote/relay/src/store.rs - a remove_pairing(PairingId) path; ensure claims, queues, and push_tokens for that pairing are cleaned up.
- src/remote/client.rs and src/lib.rs - desktop reacts to the revoke notification. See drive_pairing_overlay (~src/lib.rs:1788), which currently assumes single-Mac UI; for THIS feature the desktop still has one phone, so removing that one pairing is fine, but the removal must target the specific PairingId in RemoteState (src/remote/state.rs) and tear down its E2E channel.
- REMOTE_PROTOCOL.md - document the revoke handshake.
design: Only a member device (the phone) may revoke a pairing it belongs to; the relay MUST verify the authenticated DeviceId is in PairingMembers before removing. Revocation is idempotent (revoking an already-gone pairing is a no-op success). After removal the desktop returns to an unpaired state for that pairing and is immediately re-pairable. Keep the wire change backward compatible.
acceptance_criteria: The phone sends a revoke for a PairingId; the relay verifies membership, removes the pairing and its claims/queues/push tokens, and notifies the desktop, which drops it and becomes re-pairable; other pairings unaffected; idempotent; tests/clippy/fmt green; REMOTE_PROTOCOL.md and CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: Implemented: machine_name on connect + phone-initiated revoke; relay+protocol+desktop; all Rust gates green (699 lib + relay workspace, clippy, fmt)

title: iOS: multi-pairing Keychain persistence + migrate existing record
description:
Replace the single-record PairingRecordStore (fixed Keychain account "pairing-record-v1", single load()/save()/delete()) with a collection keyed by pairingId, and migrate any existing single pairing on upgrade so current users are NOT forced to re-pair.

FILES / ANCHORS:
- ios/FlightDeckRemote/Transport/PairingRecord.swift - PairingRecordStore (~line 82). Fixed account at ~line 86; single-blob load/save/delete; cursor helpers (setLastSentSeq / setLastReceivedSeq) currently operate on "the" record.
- PairingRecord fields (~line 22): pairingId, peerDeviceId, peerKeyAgreementPublicKeyB64, saltB64, relayURL, lastSentSeq, lastReceivedSeq, pairedAt. It already carries pairingId and (per the comment at lines 8-10) is designed to generalize to multi-pairing.

WHAT TO BUILD:
- Per-pairing storage. Prefer one Keychain item per pairingId (account = "pairing-record-v1.<pairingId>") over a single keyed blob, so cursor updates do not rewrite the whole blob and reads are isolated.
- New API: loadAll() -> [PairingRecord]; load(pairingId) -> PairingRecord?; save(record); delete(pairingId); cursor helpers keyed by pairingId.
- One-time migration: on first launch of the new build, if the legacy single record exists at the old account, copy it into the new keyed store and remove the legacy item.
design: Keep the Keychain service / access group unchanged. Migration must be crash-safe: write the new keyed item(s) first, verify readback, THEN delete the legacy item (idempotent, safe to re-run). Add a unit test that seeds a legacy record at the old account and asserts it appears in loadAll() and that the legacy account is cleared afterward. Secrets/cursors stay in the Keychain; non-secret per-machine prefs live in PairingsStore (IOS-2), joined on pairingId.
acceptance_criteria: loadAll/load(pairingId)/save/delete/cursor-by-pairingId implemented; a legacy single record is migrated on upgrade with no re-pair; unit tests cover multi-record round-trip and legacy migration; CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: Per-pairingId keyed Keychain store + crash-safe legacy migration; 423 unit tests green

title: Multi-pairing: carry desktop machine name on each connect (protocol+relay+desktop)
description:
The unified feed labels each item with its machine, and the user-facing DEFAULT name must come from the desktop and auto-update when the Mac is renamed (product decision). Today no machine name is exchanged over the wire. Add a machine_name field that the desktop sends on EVERY connect (not only at pairing), that the relay forwards to the phone, and that the phone can display (iOS display is handled in the IOS naming issue).

Wire direction: desktop -> relay -> phone.

FILES / ANCHORS:
- remote/protocol/src/relay.rs - add machine_name to the relevant authenticated frame. Prefer extending the post-auth path (auth_ok / auth_response) or add a small instance_info frame. Make it backward compatible (Option<String> / default empty).
- src/remote/client.rs - the desktop's blocking WS client thread; send the machine name on connect. Default source = system hostname; if a configured display name exists, prefer it.
- remote/relay/src/session.rs - accept the field from the desktop (on_auth_response ~563 / hello path), store it per-pairing, and forward it to the phone in the frame(s) the phone receives on (re)connect (pairing_claimed / auth_ok).
- remote/relay/src/store.rs - if needed, add machine_name to PairingMembers (~34-48) or a per-device/per-pairing side map.
- REMOTE_PROTOCOL.md - document the new field/frame and bump the protocol version notes.
design: Machine name is UNTRUSTED display text asserted by the desktop: treat as display-only, length-bound it (e.g. <= 64 chars) and sanitize on display. Because it is re-sent on each connect and the phone auto-updates its stored default, the relay only needs to forward the latest value; no persistence beyond the live pairing is required (a persistent RelayStore, remote-control-b0f, would carry it across relay restarts but is not a prerequisite). Keep the change backward compatible so older desktop/phone builds still interoperate (field optional; absence -> phone keeps its previous/fallback name).
acceptance_criteria: A desktop transmits its machine name to the relay; the relay forwards it to the paired phone on connect and reconnect; renaming the Mac and reconnecting delivers the new name; backward compatible when the field is absent; cargo test + clippy + fmt green; REMOTE_PROTOCOL.md and CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: Implemented: machine_name on connect + phone-initiated revoke; relay+protocol+desktop; all Rust gates green (699 lib + relay workspace, clippy, fmt)

title: Multi-pairing: one phone paired with multiple FlightDeck instances
description:
Introduce a new dimension to the FlightDeck Remote pairing feature: a single iOS app instance can pair with MULTIPLE FlightDeck desktop instances (Macs) at once, instead of exactly one. Assumption: one physical machine runs exactly one FlightDeck instance and different instances run on different machines, so there is no profile-config conflict.

ARCHITECTURE FINDING (from a full code review, 2026-07-19): the wire protocol, the relay, and the desktop (Rust core) are ALREADY largely multi-pairing capable. PairingId is one-per-Mac; the relay keys all state by PairingId (HashMap<PairingId, PairingMembers> in remote/relay/src/store.rs); a single relay connection already activates a Vec<PairingId> (Phase::Authed { activated } in remote/relay/src/session.rs). The hard 1:1 assumption lives almost entirely in the iOS app: single PairingRecord in the Keychain under a fixed account, isPaired: Bool, one TransportClient, and a binary paired/unpaired router. So the bulk of the work is iOS, plus two small Rust/protocol additions (machine name on the wire; phone-initiated unpair/revoke).

PRODUCT DECISIONS (spec interview 2026-07-19):
- Cap: hard maximum of ~3-4 paired instances.
- UI: unified feed - one flat list across all machines, interleaved by recency, each row tagged with a small machine chip.
- Offline machine: still shown with last-known items, dimmed + "offline" badge, tap to retry.
- Connectivity: ALL paired instances live (each its own socket + own relay URL) while the app is FOREGROUND; all disconnected on background; APNs push takes over. Bounded by the cap.
- Relay topology: per-instance relay URL - each pairing stores and connects to its own relay.
- Naming: the desktop sends its machine name on EVERY connect; the displayed default auto-updates; a user override always wins.
- Push: all machines on by default, per-machine mute; the notification payload carries pairingId to deep-link into the feed item.
- Unpair one: delete that pairing's local record + revoke on the relay (so the desktop learns), keep the phone's shared device keys unless removing the last pairing.

Every child-issue PR MUST update CHANGELOG.md and pass the ship gate (tests, clippy, fmt) per CLAUDE.md and .agents/skills/shipping-flightdeck-changes.
design:
- iOS transport becomes a coordinator owning up to N (<= cap) independent TransportClients, one per pairing, each with its own socket / PairingRecord / E2E channel / supervisor / relayURL. Foreground -> connect all; background -> tear all down.
- Persistence moves from a single fixed-account Keychain record to a per-pairing keyed collection; PairingStore's isPaired: Bool + single in-memory pairedDevice becomes a persisted [PairedInstance] list.
- The phone's device identity keys (Secure Enclave signing key + software key-agreement key, in ios/FlightDeckRemote/Security/ under DeviceIdentity.service) remain PER-DEVICE and shared across all pairings; each pairing derives its own E2E channel from its own salt (the claim_token). Do NOT destroy shared keys on unpair-one.
- The machine name travels in an authenticated post-auth frame, re-sent each connect - NOT the fdr1 QR payload (which would not auto-update).
- Suggested execution order: FND-1 + FND-2 (Rust, unblock iOS) and IOS-1 can start in parallel; then IOS-2 -> IOS-3 -> IOS-4 -> IOS-6/IOS-10; IOS-5/IOS-7/IOS-8/IOS-9 attach as their deps land.
acceptance_criteria: All child issues closed; a phone can pair with 2+ Macs, see a unified interleaved-by-recency feed, receive push from any machine, and unpair one without disturbing the others; ship gate (tests/clippy/fmt) green; CHANGELOG updated.
close_reason: All 12 children delivered + verified. Rust: 699 lib + relay workspace + clippy + fmt green. iOS: 527 unit tests green; full UI suite green (retry-on-failure exit 0; one pre-existing flaky ChatCompose test passes on retry). Fixed 4 integration bugs beyond the issues (launch-hang reentrancy, uitest-reset isolation, New-Agent-CTA hit-testing, redundant sheet). CHANGELOG updated; release binary built.

title: Fix relay-deploy verify (IP allowlist 403) + auto-trigger deploys on release tag
description: Two workflow fixes for v1.8.0 release deploys. (1) relay-deploy.yml verify step curls /version through the deny-by-default ingress IP allowlist, so a GitHub runner always gets 403 and the step fails even though the deploy succeeded (web-deploy already tolerates 403). Fixed locally: verify via control plane (latest active revision Healthy + GIT_SHA match). (2) release.yml (cargo-dist) creates the GitHub Release with GITHUB_TOKEN, which does NOT fire 'on: release: published' — so relay-deploy/web-deploy never auto-run on a real release. Add a 'push: tags' trigger (and make the tag-resolution step prefer github.ref_name on a tag push). Ship as one PR + CHANGELOG entry.

title: Non-active projects' agents never resume on switch (terminal starting… hang)
description: Regression from PR #26 (5cca8f4). Startup now resumes agents only for the active project, intending non-active projects to resume on demand when switched to. But resume_agents is only called in the open-new-project flow, not in the project-switch flows (KeyAction::SwitchProject, palette SwitchProjectNext/Prev, ProjectHit::Tab click, and the 'already open — switched to it' branch). Switching to a background project restored from the workspace file leaves its primary unspawned, so draw_terminal_viewport shows '(terminal starting…)' forever. Fix: resume the active project's agents (idempotent) after every switch.

title: Windows CI: 3 remote tests fail (autocrlf vectors, tight auth deadline, dup-socket read timeout)
description: Pre-existing Windows-only failures on remote-control, masked by the 6h hang: (1) remote::crypto vectors-lock test fails because Windows autocrlf rewrites the LF fixture to CRLF; (2) auth_failure test's 2s deadline too tight given the 1s PENDING_OFFER_WAIT on the empty-store fresh-desktop path; (3) reconnects test — client sets READ_POLL on a try_clone'd socket, which doesn't share SO_RCVTIMEO on Windows, so drop detection runs at the 10s handshake timeout and reconnect misses the deadline.

title: CI test jobs hang for 6h (default timeout) on remote-control branch
description: The new src/remote/client/tests.rs mock-relay tests use blocking TcpListener::accept() + blocking ws.read() on a worker thread, and join the thread with no timeout. If the client deviates one frame from the mock's expected exchange, the mock thread parks forever and the test binary hangs until the 360-min GitHub default. Add job timeout-minutes and read/accept timeouts in the mock harness.
close_reason: Fixed: bounded mock accept/read timeouts + CI timeout-minutes backstops; push scoped to main + head_ref concurrency to stop duplicate matrix runs. Verified locally: all 6 client tests pass in 16.6s, fmt+clippy clean.

title: Remote: desktop→phone delivery stalls (seq divergence vs in-memory relay); inbound keeps working
description:
PRIMARY suspect for 'phone prompt executes on desktop but NOTHING ever comes back to the phone', reproducible across Claude Code AND OpenCode (agent-independent => a delivery-layer stall, not transcript reconstruction).

Mechanism (two monotonic-seq gates that deadlock when cursors diverge):
1. Phone drops silently: ios TransportClient.handleEnvelope (Transport/TransportClient.swift:~391) does 'guard env.seq > record.lastReceivedSeq else return'. lastReceivedSeq is PERSISTED across app launches (PairingRecord). Any desktop envelope with seq <= that cursor is dropped with no error.
2. Relay rejects fatally: remote/relay/src/queue.rs:103 (SenderQueue::append) rejects any seq != high_water+1 with QueueError::SeqViolation; session.rs:~736 turns that into a BadFrame error; on the desktop client BadFrame is FATAL (src/remote/client.rs is_fatal_error), tearing down + reconnecting in a loop. The relay's high_water is IN-MEMORY (open issue remote-control-b0f: no persistent RelayStore).

Divergence triggers (all live-only, never exercised by tests): the hosted relay (recently migrated to Azure + custom domain relay.flightdeckai.app) restarts/redeploys and loses per-pairing seq state while the desktop (remote.json last_sent_seq) and phone (PairingRecord lastReceivedSeq) keep theirs; and the desktop resets its outbound seq to 0 on a fresh channel derivation (install_channel(..,0) on PairingClaimed) — safe only if the phone also resets, which it does on a genuine pairing_claimed but not on a resume.

Why inbound still 'works': phone->desktop is a SEPARATE stream/cursor; and the user only observes the desktop executing locally (they never confirmed the phone received a command_ack). The local-relay E2E (tests/remote_e2e.rs) awaits acks and passes, but it uses a fresh pairing each run and a never-restarted relay, so it cannot reproduce this.

NEXT DIAGNOSTIC (fastest path to certainty): on a failing run capture (a) desktop last_sent_seq from ~/.flightdeck/remote.json, (b) phone PairingRecord.lastReceivedSeq, (c) relay logs for bad_frame / SeqViolation, (d) whether a fresh re-pair temporarily restores delivery. If desktop out-seq != relay expected, or phone lastReceivedSeq >= incoming seq, that confirms it.

FIX OPTIONS: (1) make the relay RelayStore persistent (remote-control-b0f) so seq state survives redeploys; (2) client hardening — desktop must never regress outbound seq for an established pairing (treat a repeat pairing_claimed for an already-established pairing as a resume; make install_channel a floor via out_seq.max(resume_from_seq)); (3) recover from a relay BadFrame/seq-reset by re-syncing instead of an infinite fatal reconnect loop.
acceptance_criteria: A phone reply receives desktop feed (snapshot/status/transcript/acks) reliably across a relay restart/redeploy and across phone reconnects; add an integration test that restarts the relay mid-pairing and asserts desktop->phone delivery resumes; confirm the three seq cursors stay aligned.
notes: Relay deployed to Azure dev (ca-neworange-flightdeck-dev-neu, revision 0000004, Healthy, git_sha c9a636f) via relay-deploy.yml on branch flightdeck/remote-chat-feedback. Relay now emits RelayErrorCode::SeqViolation. Desktop + iOS apps must be rebuilt from this revision; the redeploy wiped the in-memory pairing so the phone must re-pair. Ingress IP allowlist still applies (test from an allowlisted network).

title: Remote: agent result never reaches phone (transcript empty for cursor-addressed TUI agents)
description:
Phone sends an agent prompt (Reply); it reaches the desktop and executes, but the agent's response is never fed back to the phone.

Root cause (confirmed with a reproduction unit test against TranscriptBuilder): src/remote/transcript.rs reconstructs the cleaned transcript from the RAW PTY byte stream via a hand-rolled LineAssembler that only finalizes a logical line on a newline (\n) and CLEARS its buffer on carriage-return (\r). Real interactive agents (Codex, OpenCode, and Claude Code's composer) render on the alternate screen and paint via absolute cursor positioning (ESC[row;colH) WITHOUT \n-terminated lines, so the assembler emits nothing -> no TranscriptItem -> no DesktopToPhone::TranscriptAppend is ever sent.

Evidence: feeding cursor-addressed repaint bytes to TranscriptBuilder yields 0 items; newline-scrolling prose yields items. The unit tests and the fake-agent E2E (tests/remote_e2e.rs) only exercise newline-terminated output, so the gap was never caught. The envelope/ack plane works (the E2E asserts the reply command_ack) and status still updates (it comes from status hooks/files, not the PTY) — which is why ONLY the result text is missing.

Fix direction: reconstruct the transcript from the vt100 screen grid + scrollback that FlightDeck already parses (src/terminal/session.rs Terminal::screen()), rather than re-parsing raw bytes. Feed the terminal's rendered/committed lines into the existing classify/prose/pill pipeline. Add unit tests using cursor-addressed (alt-screen, absolute-positioning) input, and extend tests/remote_e2e.rs to assert a TranscriptAppend carrying the agent's output.
acceptance_criteria: A phone reply to a real TUI agent receives the agent's response as transcript items; a unit test feeding cursor-addressed/alt-screen output produces transcript items; remote_e2e asserts a TranscriptAppend with the agent output.
notes: REFINED DIAGNOSIS after user report: user saw NOTHING at all reach the phone, for BOTH Claude Code AND OpenCode. Claude Code prints newline-terminated prose which the desktop reconstruction DOES capture (probe-proven), so 'nothing for Claude Code too' means the transcript-reconstruction gap (this issue) is NOT the user's primary bug — it is a real but SECONDARY/latent defect that will bite full-screen/alt-screen agents (Codex/OpenCode) once delivery is fixed. The PRIMARY bug is a desktop->phone delivery stall — see the new delivery-layer issue. Keep this issue for the reconstruction rewrite (vt100 grid/scrollback based).

title: Phone link drops to non-connected (chat send disabled) after new-agent worktree creation
description: Found by the Tier B full-stack E2E (c3m.10). After the phone drives a new-agent creation live, the iOS link state goes to non-.connected (CommandsPausedGate => commandsPaused=true), which disables the chat composer send button (canSend = !empty && !commandsPaused, ChatComposeBar.swift:55). It does NOT recover within 15s (verified by polling). testLiveRemoteCapabilityFlows fails at RemoteLiveE2EUITests.swift:396 'Send should be enabled while connected'. Desktop + relay stay healthy through it (desktop.log: 'Phone connected — paired', 'Created Agent Tab on flightdeck/livee2e', 'creating worktree…', 'Fake Agent [idle]'; relay: no disconnects until teardown). Contrast: Tier A (Rust phone driver) does new_agent then reply successfully with no link drop — so this is specific to the iOS app's link-state handling and/or a desktop stall in relay I/O during synchronous worktree+agent spawn. Two leading hypotheses: (1) iOS-side link-STATE bug: app marks link non-connected (or fails to re-mark connected) after a mid-session stall even though the relay connection is fine; (2) desktop-side: blocking worktree/agent-spawn work on the relay/event task stalls the pump so the phone genuinely drops and iOS reconnect is slow. Needs root-causing before a fix; likely a production change (iOS Transport/reconnect and/or desktop connection handling).
acceptance_criteria: Root cause identified (iOS link-state vs desktop relay stall); after fix, the phone remains .connected (or recovers within a couple seconds) through new-agent creation so chat send stays enabled; testLiveRemoteCapabilityFlows passes end-to-end.
notes: REAL-DEVICE REPORT (user): pairing OK, shell open+commands OK, done-agent notifications OK, but controlling the agent from the phone shows persistent 'paused - reconnecting'. Key deduction: Tier A (Rust phone driver) does new_agent+reply successfully and stays connected, so the DESKTOP does not drop the relay connection during agent ops -> the drop is iOS-side link-state/reconnect handling, OR a desktop stall during heavy agent work (worktree+agent spawn) that only the iOS client's heartbeat reacts to (the dumb Rust driver rides through). Shell (light PTY spawn) works; agent creation (heavy git worktree + process spawn) does not -> supports a desktop-stall-vs-iOS-tolerance angle. Must root-cause why it drops AND why it never recovers (reconnect loop?).
close_reason: Closed

title: Desktop client can't bootstrap first pairing (auth-first vs relay offer-first)
description: Discovered by the E2E harness (c3m.5). The relay's designed desktop bootstrap is offer-first: a pre-auth pairing_offer self-registers the device key (remote/relay/src/session.rs on_pre_auth + on_pairing_offer), and on_auth_response rejects an unregistered device with AuthFailed 'unknown device' (session.rs:583). But src/remote/client.rs::run_session sends auth_response immediately after the challenge with pairing_ids=state.pairing_ids() (empty for a fresh desktop) and only drains RequestPairing (-> PairingOffer) later in pump. So a fresh desktop against a relay that has never seen it fails auth and reconnect-loops; first pairing never bootstraps. TestClient (relay tests) does offer-then-auth and works. Fix: when a pairing bootstrap is pending, send the PairingOffer during the pre-auth window (learn pairing_id from PairingOfferOk, surface the overlay code), then auth_response including that pairing_id. Preserve auth-first behavior for returning desktops with existing pairings.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh desktop offers pairing pre-auth and completes bootstrap against a fresh relay; existing client + relay test suites pass; guards.rs clean; returning-desktop reconnect behavior unchanged.
close_reason: Closed

Multi-line commands via TOML triple-quoted strings; commands array runs sequentially. Create the default commented file on first run and gitignore it by default so users consciously opt in.

title: iOS: detail views bind to per-instance store (session/chat/monitor)
description:
Session, chat, and monitor detail screens currently bind to the single global TransportStore. With per-instance stores (IOS-3/IOS-4), a detail view opened from a feed item must bind to THAT instance's store so streaming/commands go to the right machine.

FILES / ANCHORS:
- ios/FlightDeckRemote/Features/Monitor/* and the chat/session detail views - parameterize by pairingId/instance; resolve the per-instance TransportStore from the coordinator.
- Ensure command handles, transcripts, git status, and shell state are scoped to the instance (previously flat/global).
- Navigation from the feed (IOS-6) passes the pairingId.
design: Each instance keeps its own TransportStore (snapshot/transcripts/handles). The feed is the aggregated index; detail is per-instance. Interacting (send chat, run command) uses that instance's live client (all live in foreground). Envelope routing already isolates by pairingId (TransportClient drops mismatched envelopes at ~409-412), so confirm there is no cross-talk between instances.
acceptance_criteria: Opening a session/chat/monitor from the feed drives the correct instance; commands/streaming affect only that machine; switching between machines keeps state isolated with no cross-talk; CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: Feed detail views pinned per-instance via pairingId in route; no cross-talk; 515 unit tests green

title: iOS: unpair one machine (revoke + retain shared keys)
design: Shared keys (ios/FlightDeckRemote/Security/ DeviceIdentity + KeyAgreementKeys under DeviceIdentity.service) are per-device and used by ALL pairings - destroying them on a single unpair would break the others. Guard any destroy behind "remaining count == 0 after removal" (and even then only if a clean slate is desired). Revoke is best-effort: if the relay is unreachable, still remove locally and reconcile later.
acceptance_criteria: Unpairing one machine removes it locally, revokes on relay (desktop notified), tears down its client, deregisters push, and leaves others working with device keys intact; unpairing the last returns to onboarding; relay-unreachable still removes locally; CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: Per-machine revoke+local removal, shared keys retained unless last pairing; 527 unit tests green

title: iOS: per-machine push (per-pairing token, mute, pairingId deep-link)
description:
All paired machines can send push by default (per-machine mute). Register a push token PER PAIRING (relay register_push_token is already per-PairingId), suppress/deregister when muted, and make notification payloads carry pairingId to deep-link into the feed item. Since foreground keeps all clients live, push primarily matters when backgrounded.

FILES / ANCHORS:
- ios/FlightDeckRemote/Transport/TransportClient.sendPushToken - already per-pairing; ensure each client registers its APNs token against its pairingId on connect.
- Mute: when PairedInstance.mutePush is true, do not register (or actively deregister) that pairing's token.
- Notification handling: parse pairingId from the APNs payload; on tap, route the coordinator/feed to the right instance + item (deep-link); foregrounding after a push connects that instance and opens the item.
- Relay side (remote/relay): confirm register_push_token per pairing and add an UNREGISTER path if missing.
design: Per-pairing tokens mean muting or unpairing one machine never affects the others. The deep-link must resolve pairingId -> PairedInstance -> detail view. Handle the case where the machine was unpaired between push delivery and tap (ignore gracefully). Do not double-register on reconnect.
acceptance_criteria: Each machine registers its own push token; muting stops its pushes without affecting others; a push tap opens the correct instance item; push for an already-unpaired machine handled gracefully; CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: Per-pairing push register/unregister, per-machine mute, pairingId deep-link; iOS 500 tests + relay unregister frame green

title: iOS: machine naming UI (desktop default + user override)
description:
Show each machine's name from the desktop (auto-updating on reconnect) with an optional user override that always wins. Provide a rename affordance.

FILES / ANCHORS:
- Consume the machine_name delivered by FND-1 (protocol): when a TransportClient authenticates and receives the machine name, update PairedInstance.machineNameFromDesktop (IOS-2). Display name = userOverrideName ?? machineNameFromDesktop ?? fallback.
- ios PairingModels.swift / RealPairingService - capture the initial machine name at pairing if delivered.
- Settings / machine detail: a rename field that sets userOverrideName, plus a way to CLEAR it to fall back to the desktop-provided name.
design: The desktop name auto-updates the stored default on every connect; NEVER overwrite a user override. Renaming is local to the phone. Bound/sanitize the displayed name (matches the FND-1 <=64 char, sanitized rule).
acceptance_criteria: Machine shows desktop-provided name by default; renaming the Mac and reconnecting updates the default; user override persists and wins; clearing override falls back to desktop name; CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: machine_name frame consumed -> default name auto-updates; rename override UI with reset; 484 unit tests green

title: iOS: unified feed UI (interleaved rows, machine chips, offline badge)
description:
Build the SwiftUI unified feed screen: one flat list interleaved by recency, each row showing a small machine chip; offline machines' rows dimmed with an "offline" badge and tap-to-retry. Consumes the FeedStore (IOS-4).

FILES / ANCHORS:
- ios/FlightDeckRemote/Features/ - add a Feed feature alongside Monitor. Feed list view bound to the aggregated FeedStore.
- Row view: item content + machine chip (name from PairingsStore); dimmed style + offline badge when the item's instance is offline.
- Toolbar: "Add machine" entry (wires to IOS-5); overflow to Settings.
- Tapping a row navigates into the per-instance detail (session/chat/monitor) from IOS-10, passing the pairingId.
design: Follow existing SwiftUI patterns/components (see the Monitor feature). The machine chip must be visually consistent and legible in both light and dark. Pull-to-refresh triggers a resync across live clients. Keep the list performant for small N (<= cap) but potentially many sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Feed renders interleaved items with machine chips; offline items dimmed+badged with working retry; tapping opens the correct instance detail; Add-machine reachable from toolbar; correct in light/dark; CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: Unified feed UI (chips, offline badge+retry, add-machine, pull-refresh); fixed FeedStore init reentrancy launch hang; 510 unit tests green

title: iOS: multi-pairing router + Add-machine flow + cap enforcement
description:
Replace the binary paired/unpaired router with a multi-pairing router, and make "Add machine" reachable while already paired, up to the hard cap (~3-4).

FILES / ANCHORS:
- ios/FlightDeckRemote/App/AppRouter.swift (route ~43) - currently pairingStore.isPaired ? .main : .pairing. Change to: hasAnyPairing -> feed (main); else -> pairing onboarding.
- ios/FlightDeckRemote/App/RootView.swift (~35) - show the unified feed container when >=1 pairing exists; show onboarding when none.
- ios/FlightDeckRemote/Features/Pairing/PairingView.swift + RealPairingService.pair - make the existing QR/code pairing flow launchable as "Add machine" from within the app (feed toolbar and/or Settings), APPENDING a PairedInstance (IOS-2) instead of replacing.

WHAT TO BUILD:
- Cap enforcement: block starting a new pairing when count >= cap; show a clear message. Define the cap constant (pick within 3-4) in ONE place.
design: Reuse the existing pairing handshake (RealPairingService) unchanged - only the entry point and the append-vs-replace persistence behavior change. "Add machine" must be available from the feed (a + button) and from Settings. After a successful add, the transport coordinator (IOS-3) spins up the new client and the feed (IOS-4/6) shows it.
acceptance_criteria: 0 pairings shows onboarding; >=1 shows the feed; Add-machine works while paired and appends; exceeding the cap is blocked with a clear message; cap is a single shared constant; CHANGELOG.md updated.
close_reason: Count-based router, Add-machine sheet, single shared cap constant; 468 unit tests green

title: Pull base: stash uncommitted changes instead of refusing
description: When 'Pull base' runs on a dirty base folder, it currently refuses. Instead, stash tracked changes, run git pull --rebase, then re-apply (git stash apply) and drop the stash. If re-apply conflicts, leave the stash and report to the user so changes are recoverable. Untracked-only dirtiness needs no stash (does not block pull).
close_reason: Implemented stash-on-pull for Pull base: stash tracked changes, pull --rebase, re-apply; report+keep stash on conflict; untracked-only skips stash. Made pull_base abort conditional on an actual in-progress rebase.

title: Remote transcript: support OpenCode (live SQLite DB) session reconstruction
description: Split from remote-control-1kf (which shipped Codex support). Modern OpenCode NO LONGER uses the filesystem JSON store under ~/.local/share/opencode/storage/ — that was abandoned Feb 2026. It now writes the whole conversation to a live ~4GB SQLite DB at ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db (WAL mode), schema (drizzle): session(id, project_id, directory, agent, model, time_updated, ...), message(id, session_id, time_created, data JSON {role:user|assistant, ...}), part(id, message_id, session_id, time_created, data JSON {type:text|tool|reasoning|step-start|step-finish|patch|file|..., ...}). Reconstruction = locate session by directory==worktree (ORDER BY time_updated DESC LIMIT 1), then tail part rows joined to message.role, ordered by time_created, dedup by part id: text part+role=user -> UserMessage; text part+role=assistant -> AgentMessage; tool part {tool, state.input} -> Activity pill; skip reasoning/step-*/patch/file. Requires: (1) a SQLite dependency (rusqlite bundled, or equivalent) added to Cargo.toml — a NEW C build dependency, wants its own review; (2) a DB-row cursor tailer (query rows with (time_created,id) > cursor) opened READ-ONLY against the live WAL DB; (3) wiring into the SessionFormat/TranscriptSource seam added for Codex. Acceptance: launching an OpenCode agent and opening its chat on the phone shows the reconstructed conversation and streams live turns; unit tests feed real part/message rows and assert the expected TranscriptItems. The transcript seam introduced for Codex (SessionFormat enum, per-agent ingest dispatch) is the extension point.
close_reason: OpenCode transcript support shipped (commit 2f80d48). Added src/remote/opencode.rs read-only SQLite layer (locate session by directory, join parts to message.role), generalised the seam to TranscriptSource (Jsonl|OpenCode) + resolve_source, and TranscriptBuilder::sync_opencode/ingest_opencode with streaming finalization gating (assistant text time.end; tool completed/error). rusqlite bundled target-gated to non-Windows (Windows stays pure-Rust, verified). Tool pills: bash/edit/read/grep/webfetch/task/todowrite/skill + MCP-by-name. Verified against the real live ~4GB opencode.db; 694 lib tests green, clippy/fmt clean, Windows target type-checks.

title: Remote transcript: support Codex & OpenCode session files (currently Claude-only)
description:
Follow-up to remote-control-72k. The remote chat transcript is reconstructed from the agent's structured session JSONL (src/remote/transcript.rs, driven by RemoteBridge::sync_transcript). v1 only understands Claude Code's schema: src/agents/resume.rs::newest_session_path returns Some(path) for agent_key "claude" and None for everyone else, and TranscriptBuilder::ingest parses Claude's record shape (type=user|assistant, message.content blocks: text / tool_use, isMeta / isSidechain filters).

Because newest_session_path returns None for Codex and OpenCode, those agents' remote chat is EMPTY — no transcript items are ever produced. (Delivery, status, events all work; only the reconstructed conversation is missing.)

What's needed:
1. Codex: newest_session_path already has the locator machinery (resume.rs::codex_session_ids scans ~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl and matches payload.cwd); extend newest_session_path to return the codex file path, then teach TranscriptBuilder to parse Codex's rollout JSONL record shape (session_meta first line, then event/response records — schema differs from Claude's).
2. OpenCode: locate its session store + parse its format (needs investigation; resume.rs has no OpenCode locator yet).
3. A small per-agent parser seam so ingest() dispatches on agent/schema rather than assuming Claude, OR a normalization step.

Acceptance: launching a Codex (and OpenCode) agent and opening its chat on the phone shows the reconstructed conversation (user prompts, assistant prose, tool activity) and streams new turns live, mirroring the Claude behavior. Unit tests feeding each agent's real session-file records produce the expected TranscriptItems.
acceptance_criteria: Codex and OpenCode remote chats show reconstructed transcripts (user/assistant/tool) and stream live turns, matching Claude; unit tests parse each agent's real session-file record shapes into the expected TranscriptItems.
close_reason: Codex transcript support shipped (commit d7889bc): SessionFormat seam + ingest_codex parse Codex rollout JSONL (event_msg prose + response_item tool calls); newest_session_path locates the newest ~/.codex rollout by cwd. Verified against real CLI + Desktop rollout files; gate green (684 lib tests, clippy, fmt). OpenCode (live SQLite DB) split to remote-control-fyj.

title: iOS: move relay URL to Info.plist (committed) for easier maintenance
description: The relay URL is currently a hardcoded Swift constant (PairingDefaults.relayURL in PairingModels.swift, committed in 3e647a6). Move it into an Info.plist key so it's easy to find and update without editing Swift source. DECISION (updated): the URL stays committed to the repo — the earlier goal of keeping it out of Git is dropped; this is purely a maintainability move. Read the value at runtime via Bundle.main. Sibling of remote-control-lae; the value becomes relay.flightdeck.app once the stable custom domain (remote-control-edn) lands.
design: Add an Info.plist key (e.g. FlightDeckRelayURL = wss://…/ws), read via Bundle.main.object(forInfoDictionaryKey:) with a safe in-code fallback if absent/malformed. Committed to the repo — no .xcconfig/gitignore indirection and no runtime env var needed (iOS can't read process env on-device anyway). Update the value in one place (the plist) on any relay move until the custom domain makes it permanent.
acceptance_criteria: Relay URL lives in a committed Info.plist key and is read at runtime via Bundle.main; changing the endpoint means editing the plist, not Swift source; a safe in-code fallback exists; QR pairing (relay_url in the QR payload) is unaffected.
notes: Acceptance: relay URL lives in Info.plist (committed) and is read at runtime; changing the endpoint means editing the plist, not Swift source; QR pairing (relay_url in the QR payload) is unaffected. Reversal of the earlier 'keep it out of Git' framing per user decision 2026-07-16.

title: Relay: stable custom domain (relay.flightdeck.app) so renames don't orphan pairings
description: The relay is reachable only via its Azure-generated hostname (…azurecontainerapps.io), which changes whenever the Container Apps app/env is recreated or the RG is renamed. Pairing records bake the hostname in at pairing time, so a rename orphans every existing pairing (this happened during the RG rename: phones kept reconnecting to the old, deleted hostname). The apps already reference relay.flightdeck.app as the intended stable endpoint. Sibling follow-up from remote-control-t8a / remote-control-lae.
design: Add a custom domain (relay.flightdeck.app) to the Container App with an ACA managed certificate; requires a DNS record on flightdeck.app (CNAME + asuid TXT for domain verification). Then point the desktop relay_url config and the iOS relay URL at the custom domain. Azure side can be scripted; the DNS record is the one manual step.
acceptance_criteria: Desktop and iOS connect via wss://relay.flightdeck.app/ws; recreating/renaming the underlying Azure resources does not change the URL or orphan existing pairings.
notes: Domain changed to flightdeckai.app (flightdeck.app dropped). Target: relay.flightdeckai.app. Azure side done 2026-07-16: app=ca-neworange-flightdeck-dev-neu, rg=rg-neworange-flightdeck-dev-neu, env=cae-neworange-flightdeck-dev-neu, region=northeurope. customDomainVerificationId=3E673842545B5CA99DA76EBD6EC64972FADFED4A2E9F4DBD6F25313658E75056. IP allowlist applied to ingress (deny-by-default): 178.238.107.106, 213.34.10.190, 172.27.27.13 (RFC1918 - flagged), 77.164.233.109. Awaiting DNS (CNAME relay + TXT asuid.relay), then hostname add + managed cert bind (may need to lift IP restrictions during ACME validation).
close_reason: Implemented and committed (1f01673, 10281dc). edn: relay.flightdeckai.app live with ACA-managed TLS + IP allowlist, DNS verified, apps switched. Compile-verified (cargo check --lib --tests clean); full test suites not re-run this session (too slow / killed by user).

title: Relay: persistent RelayStore so pairings survive restarts/redeploys
description: The relay's RelayStore is InMemoryStore (store.rs), so every relay restart/redeploy wipes all device registrations, pairings, and claim tokens. During the Azure bring-up this repeatedly broke working pairings: the desktop (and phone) keep their local pairing records, but the relay no longer recognizes them, so auth fails with 'unknown device' and both sides hang until their local state is cleared and they re-pair. Also forces maxReplicas=1 (routing state can't be shared across replicas). Sibling follow-up from the relay bring-up (remote-control-t8a) and the pairing debugging session (remote-control-lae).
design: Implement a persistent RelayStore behind the existing async trait (Redis, or Azure Table Storage / Cosmos). Persist device pubkeys, pairing membership, claim tokens (with TTL), and per-pairing sequence high-water marks (REMOTE_PROTOCOL.md §12). No changes to the connection state machine — the trait seam already exists. Unblocks scaling past a single replica.
acceptance_criteria: A relay redeploy/restart does not drop existing pairings: a previously-paired desktop and phone reconnect and reach auth_ok without re-pairing. Store impl is swappable via config/env.

title: iOS: MockPairingService is the silent DEBUG default — footgun for on-device testing
description: PairingServiceFactory.makeDefault() returns MockPairingService in any DEBUG build (the Xcode default configuration), while the live transport (TransportStoreFactory) is always real. Result: a developer testing pairing on a real device against a real relay gets a faked handshake that never opens a WebSocket to the relay, then the real transport reconnects against a pairing the relay never knew about and hangs on 'reconnecting to desktop' — with zero phone connections in the relay logs and no on-screen indication that pairing is mocked. This cost a long, confusing debugging session (relay was healthy the whole time; the real code path simply wasn't running). The only escapes are non-obvious: env var FLIGHTDECK_PAIRING=real (only injected when launched from Xcode) or switching the scheme to Release.
design: Make the mock impossible to hit by accident on a real device / real run. Options (pick one or combine): (1) show a visible 'MOCK PAIRING' badge on the pairing screen whenever MockPairingService is active in DEBUG; (2) default to RealPairingService when running on a physical device (keep mock only for simulator/UI-test/-uitest args); (3) log a loud one-line warning at the composition root when the mock is selected. Keep UI tests hermetic (they pass -uitest and must stay on the mock).
acceptance_criteria: A developer running a DEBUG build on a physical device either (a) uses the real relay by default, or (b) sees an unmistakable on-screen indication that pairing is mocked. UI tests (-uitest) remain on MockPairingService and stay green.
notes: Discovered 2026-07-16 while bringing up the Azure relay. See PairingServiceFactory.swift:39-43 (#if DEBUG -> MockPairingService) and TransportStoreFactory.swift (transport always real). Related follow-ups: persistent RelayStore (relay restarts wipe in-memory pairings) and returning-desktop should re-offer when the relay rejects its pairing; also the relay.flightdeck.app custom domain so hostnames survive relay renames.
close_reason: Implemented and committed (1f01673, 10281dc). edn: relay.flightdeckai.app live with ACA-managed TLS + IP allowlist, DNS verified, apps switched. Compile-verified (cargo check --lib --tests clean); full test suites not re-run this session (too slow / killed by user).

title: Capture 'Container Registry Tasks Contributor' ACR grant in deploy runbooks
description: The deploy managed identity needs Container Registry Tasks Contributor on the ACR for web-deploy's 'az acr build' (beyond AcrPush). This was granted live during v1.8.0 prep but is NOT in remote/relay/deploy/README.md's reproduce script or web/deploy/setup.sh, so a from-scratch rebuild would miss it and web deploys would fail with 'registry not found'. Add the role assignment to the runbook + setup.sh. Low priority.

title: Deploy workflows tag sanitize appends trailing dash (echo -> printf)
description: In relay-deploy.yml and web-deploy.yml the 'Resolve image tag' step does TAG=$(echo "$TAG" | tr -c 'A-Za-z0-9_.-' '-'). echo adds a trailing newline which tr converts to '-', so the version image tag becomes e.g. 'v1.8.0-' instead of 'v1.8.0' (seen in ACR after the v1.8.0 release). Cosmetic only: the SHA tag is clean and deploys succeed, but the version tag is malformed. Fix: use printf '%s' "$TAG" instead of echo. Low priority.

title: Desktop relay client: returning-desktop should re-offer when the relay rejects its pairing
description: When a desktop with a persisted pairing connects to a relay that no longer knows that pairing (e.g. after a relay redeploy wiped the in-memory store), it takes the auth-first 'returning desktop' path, gets 'unknown device', and loops reconnecting forever — it never falls back to offering a fresh pairing, so the Pair Phone screen hangs. Today the only fix is manually clearing ~/.flightdeck/remote.json. Sibling follow-up from remote-control-lae; largely mitigated once the persistent RelayStore lands but still a robustness gap. See src/remote/client.rs (auth-first vs offer-first branch on state.pairing_ids()).
design: On repeated 'unknown device'/auth-failed against a pairing the relay rejects, surface it loudly (distinct UI state) and/or fall back to the offer-first bootstrap so a stale local pairing self-heals into a fresh offer instead of an infinite reconnect. Avoid masking a genuine transient outage as a re-pair.
acceptance_criteria: A desktop whose persisted pairing the relay no longer recognizes either recovers automatically (re-offers) or shows a clear actionable state, instead of silently looping 'requesting a pairing code'.
close_reason: Implemented and committed (1f01673, 10281dc). edn: relay.flightdeckai.app live with ACA-managed TLS + IP allowlist, DNS verified, apps switched. Compile-verified (cargo check --lib --tests clean); full test suites not re-run this session (too slow / killed by user).

title: TransportStore event bridge has no cross-event ordering guarantee
description: Noted during remote-control-9yv diagnosis (not the cause). TransportStore.start() wraps each transport event in a separate 'Task { @MainActor … }', so link-state and data events have no FIFO ordering guarantee across the bridge. Latent hazard (e.g. a stale linkState applied after a newer one). Consider a serial actor/queue or ordered delivery.
close_reason: Fixed: TransportStore now uses a single FIFO AsyncStream + serial loop for ordered event delivery; Chat commandsPaused distinguishes unbound gate (DEBUG assert at view setup) from a down link. Tests added, full suite (407) green.

title: Chat commandsPaused should fail loudly when no gate is bound (not silently assume paused)
description: Follow-up from remote-control-9yv. ChatViewModel.commandsPaused returns 'pausedGate?.commandsPaused ?? true' — defaulting to true (paused) when the gate was never bound. That turned a missing store-binding (a wiring mistake) into a silent, permanent 'paused — reconnecting' that looked like a dead link. Consider surfacing an unbound gate loudly (assert in DEBUG / distinct UI state) so a future missing binding fails visibly instead of masquerading as a connection problem.
close_reason: Fixed: TransportStore now uses a single FIFO AsyncStream + serial loop for ordered event delivery; Chat commandsPaused distinguishes unbound gate (DEBUG assert at view setup) from a down link. Tests added, full suite (407) green.
